codeReview.summary: The commit removes timeout masking by stubbing DeFiLlama calls in `tests/compare-coingecko.test.ts`, but it does not fully satisfy the feature contract because the handoff commit does not contain the live onchain catalog implementation it claims to preserve and prove. The only code change is a test-only isolation tweak; the live catalog/runtime behavior remained in earlier commits, so the assigned requirement that the feature commit fully contain the claimed implementation is not met.

issues.description: Commit `3aebd7e` changes only compare-suite test setup/teardown. It does isolate the expensive DeFiLlama fetches, but it does not itself contain the live onchain pool/catalog implementation that the feature description says must be fully contained in the handoff commit. `git show --stat 3aebd7e` reports a single-file test-only diff, so scrutiny cannot validate the claimed runtime/catalog implementation from this feature commit alone.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The `onchain-api-worker` procedure appears to allow a worker to hand off a fix feature whose commit contains only test isolation even when the feature description also requires commit-contained runtime behavior. That made this session declare success despite the commit not fully containing the claimed implementation.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Feature description in mission `features.json` requires: 'Ensure the handoff commit fully contains the claimed implementation.' Handoff for session `1cd27a87-e02f-44d5-ac1f-843dfc779fe2` says onchain route behavior stayed covered, but `git show --stat 3aebd7e` shows only `tests/compare-coingecko.test.ts` changed.

summary: Reviewed the feature handoff, transcript skeleton, current HEAD, and commit `3aebd7e`. The timeout-padding regression is addressed by compare-suite DeFiLlama stubbing, but the review fails because the feature explicitly required the handoff commit to fully contain the claimed implementation and this commit is test-only.
